turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Reporting an Internet Outage at Your Housing

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Hello, thanks for calling. How can I help you today?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Hi, good morning. I’m calling because my internet is not working, like completely, and I don’t know if it’s only me or the building, and I’m a bit… eh… worried because I need it for class.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I understand, that’s stressful. Let’s check it together. When did you first notice the internet stopped working?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Last night, more or less at… maybe ? I was watching a lecture and it just stopped, and then I tried again and it was like, no connection. I mean, no internet.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Okay, around  last night. Thank you. Are you using Wi-Fi, or a cable connection?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Wi-Fi. I don’t use cable, I don’t have it. And my phone also no internet, so it’s not only my laptop.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it, multiple devices are affected. That suggests it’s not a single device issue. Can you look at your modem or router for me? Are there any lights on, and what colours are they?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, I’m looking now. The power light is on. The other one, the internet one, is red, and it’s blinking like… blink blink. Not stable.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s very helpful. A blinking red internet light usually means the connection to the service is down. Have you tried restarting the modem or router?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, I did. I unplugged it. First I waited one minute, then I did again and I waited like five minutes because someone told me you need to wait more. But it came back the same, red blinking.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: You did exactly the right steps. Thank you for trying that already. Do you live in an apartment building with shared internet, or do you have your own service in your unit?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: I think it’s my own service, but also the building has… how you say… like the same company for many people, I think. I’m not sure.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s okay. We can still check outages in your area. Can you confirm your address for me, including the unit number?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, it’s 18 King Street, apartment 304. And the postcode is 2000.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Thank you. I’m checking now. Just one moment. Okay, I can see there’s a known outage reported in your building area from last night. That means you’re not the only one.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Ah, okay. That makes me feel better, because I was thinking maybe I broke something. So it’s general, yes?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, it looks like a network issue impacting multiple customers nearby. It’s currently marked as “in progress” for repair.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: In progress means they are fixing now? Or it means later? Sorry, I don’t understand.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Good question. “In progress” means technicians are actively working on it now, but it’s not yet resolved.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay, so they are working now. Do you have an estimate time? Like, more or less.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: The current estimated restoration time is within the next three hours. I know that’s a wide window, but it’s the best information we have right now.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Three hours… hmm. I have a Zoom at 1 p.m. If it’s not fixed, what can I do? Like, can you give me a solution?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I understand. While the outage is ongoing, the main options are using mobile data as a hotspot, or going to a place with Wi-Fi, like your campus library or a café.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes, I can use hotspot, but my data is not so much. And if I use too much, it’s expensive. So I want to ask, can I get discount for this outage?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Yes, you can request a service credit once the outage is resolved, since you’ve been without service. I can also note your account right now that you called during the outage.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Yes please, because sometimes when you call later they say, “no record”, and I don’t want that.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Completely understandable. I’ve added a note to your account with the time of your call and the outage reference.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Thank you. Another question, sorry, I have many questions. If the internet comes back and then goes again, should I restart again, or should I not touch it?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That’s a good question. If it comes back and drops again, you can restart once. But if it keeps happening, it’s better to call us again rather than repeatedly restarting.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. And do you know if they need to come inside my apartment? Because I don’t want someone entering when I’m not there.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: For a building-level outage, technicians usually work on external lines or building equipment, not inside individual apartments. If an in-unit visit is needed later, we would contact you to schedule it.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay, that’s good. Because I was thinking, maybe they come and I’m not ready, you know.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Totally understandable. Can I also confirm the best contact method for updates? We can send SMS or email notifications for outages.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Email is better. I check email always. Phone I miss sometimes, because I’m in class.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: No problem. I’ll set email as your preferred contact.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Thank you. And sorry, one more thing. The modem light is red now. If the outage is fixed, will it automatically become normal? Or I need to restart again?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: In most cases it will reconnect automatically once service is restored. If it doesn’t reconnect within about 15 minutes after the outage is marked resolved, then you should restart it once.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay, 15 minutes after. That is clear.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Great. Is there anything else you’d like me to check while I have your account open?

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Umm, yes, maybe. If I use hotspot for Zoom, does it use too much? Like, how much data?

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: It can use quite a bit, especially if your camera is on. If you need to conserve data, you can turn off video and use audio only, and reduce screen sharing where possible.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Ah yes, I can turn off camera. That’s a good tip.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I’m glad. I’m really sorry again for the inconvenience. We’ll keep working to restore service as quickly as possible.

turns.speaker: learner
turns.text: Okay. Thank you for your patience, and for explaining. I will wait, and if not fixed I call again.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: That sounds good. Thank you for calling, and I hope it’s back very soon.
